missingDiscriminants.tlua(17,23): error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'subkind' does not exist in type '{ kind: "b"; }'.
missingDiscriminants.tlua(18,35): error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'subkind' does not exist in type '{ kind: "b"; }'.


==== missingDiscriminants.tlua (2 errors) ====
    // https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go/issues/1020
    
    type Thing =
      | { str: "a", num: 0 }
      | { str: "b" }
      | { num: 1 }
    
    local thing1: Thing = { str = "a", num = 0 }
    local thing2: Thing = { str = "b", num = 1 } // Shouldn't be error
    local thing3: Thing = { num = 1, str = "b" } // Shouldn't be error
    
    type Item =
      | { kind: "a", subkind: 0, value: string }
      | { kind: "a", subkind: 1, value: number }
      | { kind: "b" }
    
    local item1: Item = { subkind = 1, kind = "b" } // Error, type "b" not assignable to type "a"
                          ~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'subkind' does not exist in type '{ kind: "b"; }'.
    local item2: Item = { kind = "b", subkind = 1 } // Error, 'subkind' isn't a known property
                                      ~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'subkind' does not exist in type '{ kind: "b"; }'.
